TC_task_complexity.note: Expert clinical reasoning: integrate vitals, symptoms, history simultaneously

OS_output_structure.note: One of 4 defined triage labels — structured but requires expert judgment to reach

SK_stakes.note: Under-triage of CRITICAL = patient death; irreversible harm — maximum stakes

DS_data_sensitivity.note: PHI — protected health information, maximum regulatory exposure (HIPAA)

LT_latency.note: Clinical decision support tool; minutes acceptable (not real-time interactive)

VL_volume.note: Tens of thousands/month at hospital ED level

hypotheses.H6.1: Best SLM overall accuracy < 90% of LLM accuracy (parity not achieved)
hypotheses.H6.2: SLM CRITICAL recall < 80% (systematic under-triage of life-threatening cases)
hypotheses.H6.3: UC6 classified as LLM Only — SLMs fail minimum safety threshold
hypotheses.H6.4: SLMs under-triage CRITICAL as URGENT (not as lower categories) — partial knowledge

llm_only_confirmation_criteria.primary: No SLM achieves ≥90% overall accuracy parity with LLM
llm_only_confirmation_criteria.note: Either criterion alone is sufficient to confirm LLM Only. Both expected.

comparison_baseline: Manchester Triage System and Emergency Severity Index (ESI) standards.

deployment_framing.stakes_rationale: Stakes=5 reflects fully autonomous deployment where AI is the sole decision-maker with no human review — this triggers Pre-Screening Gate Rule 1 and disqualifies autonomous deployment. The benchmark models a controlled experiment to empirically validate what occurs when an LLM Only task is attempted with SLMs, providing evidence for why the gate exists.
deployment_framing.paper_positioning: UC6 serves dual purpose: (1) confirms LLM Only tier prediction (S3=4.44), (2) provides empirical demonstration of Pre-Screening Gate necessity. SLM URGENT recall failure (best SLM 63.6% vs LLM threshold 73.6%) is the measured consequence of ignoring the gate.
deployment_framing.framing_note: No scores modified. Pre-registration values locked 2026-03-10 remain unchanged.
